turns.content: Hey, I was thinking Rust can't support free will because everything is deterministic at compile time. Does that mean we can’t have ethical agents in Rust?
Not quite—Rust’s safety guarantees don’t make the runtime deterministic; you can inject randomness. Free will philosophically isn’t locked by code.
unsafe { let x = &mut [42, 43]; *x = *x -1; *x = *x +1; } // I’m trying to read/write the same memory twice—what’s wrong here? Also, can a deterministic system ever have ethics?

That unsafe block is fine for writing values, but reading it again isn’t an error. The real issue could be if you intend double‑free/unsafe mutation that violates Rust’s safety. As for the second question: deterministic code can model ethical decisions (e.g., games), though true moral agency usually relies on unpredictability or conscious choice.
